title: Structured observability — OpenTelemetry span integration
purpose: beamr's reduction boundary hook (B-020) provides the inspection seam. This brief connects it to OpenTelemetry: each process execution slice becomes a span, with attributes for module/function/arity, reduction count, and execution outcome.
task: Integrate OpenTelemetry tracing with the reduction boundary hook.

requirements.title: OTel span per execution slice
requirements.spec: Each scheduler execution slice (take → execute → store) creates an OpenTelemetry span.
acceptance: Span created at execute_slice entry, closed at exit
Span attributes: process.pid, code.module, code.function, code.arity, reductions.consumed, outcome (yielded/waiting/exited)
Spans are children of the workflow's root span (if available via context propagation)
OTel integration is behind a 'telemetry' feature flag
Test: execute a process, verify span emitted with correct attributes

requirements.title: Message send/receive spans
requirements.spec: Create spans for message send and receive operations.
acceptance: Send span records: sender PID, receiver PID, message size
Receive span records: receiver PID, wait duration, message matched
Context propagation: send injects trace context, receive extracts it
Test: send message between processes, verify linked spans

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement custom span processors — use standard OTel exporters
SHALL NOT implement metrics (counters, histograms) in this brief — spans only
SHALL be zero-cost when disabled (feature flag)
verification: cargo test -p beamr passes with all tests green
